title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Language Lens: Upgrade Handshake
summary: A client sends new-protocol bytes before acceptance, a server parses tunneled data as another HTTP request, buffered bytes disappear during handoff, and rejected transitions desynchronize the connection. Planned artifact: Add and verify the upgrade handshake increment in confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
State that TLS protects a connection hop but does not provide application authorization, safe methods, trusted intermediaries, malware safety, or end-to-end storage secrecy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Upgrade Handshake · Theory Upgrade Handshake
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8605 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Upgrade Handshake contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Upgrade Handshake evidence · Theory Upgrade Handshake
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-upgrade-handshake-xun1sm" that uses case 5702 to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
